parentQuestion: Which structure is considered a modified sympathetic ganglion that releases horm...

scaffolds.q: The adrenal glands sit atop the kidneys and have two distinct regions.
scaffolds.explain: Each adrenal gland has an outer cortex (produces steroid hormones — cortisol, aldosterone, androgens) and an inner medulla (produces catecholamines — epinephrine, norepinephrine). the medulla is the sympathetic extension. Open yourself to this:two glands in one.

scaffolds.q: Epinephrine (adrenaline) can act on tissues throughout the body when released into the bloodstream.
scaffolds.explain: Hormones travel in the blood to reach distant targets. when the adrenal medulla releases epinephrine, it circulates everywhere, binding to adrenergic receptors throughout the body. This creates a systemic, sustained sympathetic effect beyond what nerve terminals alone could achieve. Amplification by circulation.
